articles.title: Resurrection
articles.author: Lucy S. Burnham
articles.complete_text:
Dearest mother, Spring is here,
The birds are all returning
With wondrous songs of great delight,
For shame their hearts are yearning.

As youthful Nature hush scarce dared
To breathe,
So great her joy to hear the birds,
Hard clouds might make them leave.

I hear their sweet notes calling,
When from my bed I rise,
I see the flowers peeping,
I note in glad surprise

The Easter Lily's petals
Burst forth as if to bloom.
Sweet emblem of the coming
Of Christ from out the tomb.

My heart so lone and saddened,
Takes courage at the sight,
My weary brain seemed maddened,
All through the winter night.

Where'er I looked, dear mother,
Death stared me in the face,
I could not even dream of thee
Except in Death's embrace.

And wondrous peace steals o'er me,
A banished is the gloom.
I know that thy dear body
That now lies in the tomb

Will come forth as these petals,
To bloom in life and love,
And you'll be there to greet me
When I reach that home above.
articles.notes: This is a complete Easter/resurrection themed poem that appears in the back matter but is clearly standalone content, not advertising or misc material

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: front matter with magazine cover and contents page, advertisers directory, General Board updates about Mrs. Wells' 90th birthday celebration and social work committee activities, back matter advertisements for various Utah businesses (garments, artificial limbs, jewelry, banking, etc.), and a picture gallery showing Relief Society stake presidents. All of this is appropriate misc content that should not be extracted as standalone articles.
